title: host ABI finalized
manager: The boundary every runtime implements — the host ABI — gets frozen, now including v13's effect and persistence surface. This is the contract for exactly how a host provides hooks, sensors, channels, time, and randomness to a machine, how errors and cancellation and rollback are represented, and how replay injects recordings. Freezing it is what makes "write once, run on any host" real: a runtime that implements this ABI level runs any machine at that level.
cs: ABI = hook/sensor/channel call shapes, time + random sources, error representation, cancellation, rollback notification, replay injection, recording format; versioned; adapters declare ABI level; incorporates the era-4 durability surface (W9.14).
